disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Thick greasy yellow scalp crust plus greasy yellow scaly patches in the seborrhoeic distribution and macerated weeping behind the ears, worsened by applied oils
trigger_question_examples: Is it thick greasy yellow crust on the scalp with greasy scaly patches on the face, and have you been putting oils on it?
Is the skin behind the ears red and weeping?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, thick greasy yellow crust on the scalp, greasy scaly patches on the face, and red weeping skin behind the ears — we've been applying olive/coconut oil.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No thick honey-crusting, spreading hot/hard redness, fever, or refusal to feed / lethargy (secondary-infection & unwell-infant screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any golden crusts or pus, redness spreading across the neck/chest, a fever, or the baby refusing feeds / very floppy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No golden crusts or pus, the redness isn't spreading to the neck/chest, no fever, and he's still feeding (just fussy).

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No petechiae/purpura, no failure to thrive or systemic illness (serious-underlying-condition screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any tiny blood spots (petechiae) or bruising in the rash, or is the baby otherwise unwell/not growing?
Is the rash purpuric or associated with other symptoms?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blood spots or bruising, and he's otherwise growing and well apart from being uncomfortable.
